prompt: Add keyboard shortcuts to the task board: n=new task, arrows=navigate, enter=open detail, escape=close, d=delete with confirm, p+1-4=priority, /=focus filter bar (new). Trello/Linear style. No browser shortcut conflicts. ?=help overlay. React + Zustand.

assertions.name: has_all_sections
assertions.description: Spec contains all required sections from the template

assertions.name: problem_explains_why
assertions.criteria: The Problem section explains WHY keyboard shortcuts matter (power user efficiency, accessibility, competitive parity with Trello/Linear) — not just 'add shortcuts'
assertions.description: Problem section articulates motivation and impact

assertions.name: references_real_files
assertions.criteria: Technical Approach references at least 2 real files from the codebase (e.g., TaskBoard.tsx, TaskCard.tsx, taskStore.ts)

assertions.name: makes_architectural_decisions
assertions.criteria: The spec makes concrete decisions about: event handling strategy (global listener vs per-component), focus management approach, and how the filter bar integrates with existing board. Decisions are stated, not left as open questions.

assertions.name: verification_is_testable
assertions.criteria: Verification section has specific testable behaviors (e.g., 'pressing n creates task in focused column', 'shortcuts disabled when input focused') — not vague items
assertions.description: Verification items are concrete and testable

assertions.name: has_alternatives
assertions.criteria: Alternatives Considered section has at least one real alternative approach with reasoning for rejection
assertions.description: At least one substantive alternative considered

assertions.name: no_json_shapes
assertions.criteria: The spec does NOT contain verbatim JSON payload definitions, interface definitions, or schema shapes.

assertions.name: no_mechanism_tables
assertions.criteria: The spec does NOT contain exhaustive tables mapping every shortcut to exact handler functions, event codes, and wiring details. A table of shortcuts and their behaviors is fine; a table prescribing exact implementation is over-specification.
